page: Amazon Web Services m8in.32xlarge is an x86_64 architecture general-purpose server powered by dedicated Intel processors. The instance features 128 virtual central processing units derived from 64 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled at two threads per core. Operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz, the processors run on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, ensuring dedicated compute resource allocation without virtualization overhead.

The server is equipped with 512.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via external Elastic Block Store volumes. Network capabilities are a key feature of this instance type, which delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps, optimized for high-throughput network and EBS operations.

The absence of local NVMe storage and dedicated GPU hardware represents a clear tradeoff, making this server unsuitable for local-disk-intensive databases or hardware-accelerated machine learning workloads. However, the high-density memory configuration combined with 200 Gbps network bandwidth offers qualitative cost efficiency for large-scale distributed applications, enterprise application servers, and network-heavy workloads that rely on EBS-backed storage.
description: Amazon Web Services m8in.32xlarge is a general-purpose, network-optimized server powered by dedicated Intel processors on the x86_64 architecture. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 512.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not contain local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. It is designed with a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps, optimizing both network and EBS performance. This profile makes the instance suitable for enterprise applications, network-intensive workloads, and distributed systems requiring high-speed data transfer and substantial memory capacity without the need for local physical disks or graphics acceleration.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8in.32xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, a 3.9 GHz Intel processor, and 200 Gbps network bandwidth, optimized for EBS and network-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8in.32xlarge is an Intel-powered general-purpose server with 128 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
tagline: An Intel-powered general-purpose server featuring high-speed dedicated compute, balanced memory allocation, and optimized two-hundred gigabit baseline network bandwidth.
bullet_points: 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores powered by dedicated 3.9 GHz Intel processors
512.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for high-throughput workloads
Nitro hypervisor architecture with zero local storage and no GPU accelerators
Suited for enterprise applications, network-heavy tasks, and EBS-backed databases
